thesis: WaterBridge owns a scaled produced-water network with long-duration fixed-fee contracts and substantial dedicated acreage, but equity value depends on whether new cohorts earn attractive returns after maintenance capital and debt funding.

explanation_contracts.mechanism: WaterBridge owns a scaled produced-water network with long-duration fixed-fee contracts and substantial dedicated acreage, but equity value depends on whether new cohorts earn attractive returns after maintenance capital and debt funding.
explanation_contracts.distinguishing_test: Publish project-cohort invested capital and realized after-tax cash return.
explanation_contracts.counter_explanation: Reported EBITDA growth may be capital-purchased growth whose return falls below the cost of capital, while aggregate contract descriptions overstate cash-flow protection.
explanation_contracts.falsifier: Mature project cohorts earn below the cost of capital.
explanation_contracts.valuation_link: Primary method: normalized infrastructure cash flow and enterprise value.

open_questions: Project-cohort invested capital, utilization and after-tax cash returns are not disclosed.
Customer-level MVC, dedication, renewal and termination economics are not disclosed.
Actual covenant headroom and a stressed sources-and-uses bridge are not disclosed.
